name: Emily Brontë
biography: Emily Brontë was an English novelist and poet, best known for her only novel, Wuthering Heights, a classic of English literature.

name: Jane Austen
biography: Jane Austen was an English novelist known for her witty social commentary and works such as Pride and Prejudice and Emma.

name: Charlotte Brontë
biography: Charlotte Brontë was an English novelist and poet, author of Jane Eyre, and the eldest of the Brontë sisters.

name: Gillian Flynn
biography: Gillian Flynn is an American author and screenwriter, famous for her dark psychological thrillers, including Gone Girl.

name: Jacqueline Harpman
biography: Jacqueline Harpman was a Belgian writer whose works often explore existential and philosophical themes, including I Who Have Never Known Men.

name: Mary Shelley
biography: Mary Shelley was an English novelist, best known for writing Frankenstein, a landmark work of Gothic literature and science fiction.

name: Anne Brontë
biography: Anne Brontë was an English novelist and poet, known for The Tenant of Wildfell Hall and Agnes Grey, focusing on social issues and women's rights.

name: Marina Dyachenko
biography: Marina Dyachenko is a Ukrainian author of fantasy and science fiction novels, including Ritual, often blending folklore with imaginative storytelling.

name: Suzanne Collins
biography: Suzanne Collins is an American author and television writer, best known for The Hunger Games trilogy.

name: Marge Piercy
biography: Marge Piercy is an American poet, novelist, and social activist, known for feminist and speculative fiction, including Woman on the Edge of Time.
